turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Sorry, excuse me, please, I made a problem when I ate the lunch in the restaurant near in the Melbourne Central, and I don't know when I lost my passport. I lost the whole wallet, and in it there is $200, and my passport, and my credit card.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I’m really sorry to hear that. Can you tell me exactly where and when you last remember having your wallet — was it inside the restaurant, or somewhere after you left?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I don't know why I lost it, but when I walk outside and return my apartment, I found there is no wallet.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, I understand. We’ll try to sort this out. Could you tell me the name of the restaurant where you had lunch, or describe roughly where it’s located near Melbourne Central?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Uh, yes, I know the name of that restaurant. It's named, uh... Kallaghan. And it's an Indian restaurant. Not India. Uh... Southeast restaurant. It's located in the ground floor.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it, thanks — Kallaghan, on the ground floor near Melbourne Central. Did you already contact the restaurant to ask if anyone found your wallet, or would you like help with what to say when you call them?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: No. I don't know. I don't know what should I do. Maybe I should call the restaurant, but where I can find them?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s okay — you’re doing the right thing asking for help. You can find the restaurant’s phone number by searching “Kallaghan restaurant Melbourne Central” on Google or Maps. When you call, you can say: “Hi, I had lunch there today and I think I lost my wallet. Could you please check if anyone found it?” Would you like me to show you what to do next if they don’t have your wallet?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Well, now you are the policeman. I ask you for help.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Of course — I’ll take your report. Can you tell me your full name, please?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: My full name is Lucas Mao, and in my passport is another name, but in Melbourne, I always use Lucas.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Thank you, Lucas. Could you tell me when exactly you noticed your wallet was missing — about what time today?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: in 4 p.m. maybe from 2 or 4 p.m. because when I go outside from my apartment I use the wallet's apartment card to open my house and take the lift and when I return I don't find it

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, around 2 to 4 p.m., understood. Could you describe your wallet for me — its color, size, or any special marks or cards inside besides your passport and credit card?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: The black... white... but it doesn't have some special point. Uh... it's... the size is similar with my hand.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, a black-and-white wallet, about hand-sized. Besides your passport, credit card, and $200, did it also have anything like your driver’s license, student card, or apartment access card inside?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Students have apartment card, but the card is no name, but my student card has my picture.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it, thank you. Which school or university issued your student card, Lucas?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: R.M.I.T. University

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Thanks. That helps. And just to confirm — your passport was inside the wallet, correct? Do you remember which country it was issued by?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: They come from my passport inside my wallet, and it's Chinese.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Understood — a Chinese passport inside the wallet. Finally, could you please tell me your current address and a phone number where we can contact you if the wallet is found?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: My address is Queen Street, Melbourne, 115-7, United 2912. My phone number is 0409662419919.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Thanks, Lucas. For your safety, I can’t record or file real police reports, but here’s what you should do right now: Go to the nearest police station — the Melbourne East Police Station on Flinders Lane is closest to Melbourne Central. Tell them:“I lost my wallet near Melbourne Central around 2–4 p.m. It has my Chinese passport, RMIT student card, apartment card, credit card, and about $200.” Bring any ID or documents you still have. They’ll give you an incident report number, which you can use to cancel your cards and apply for a passport replacement at the Chinese consulate. Would you like me to show you how to tell the police about it step-by-step in English? 窗体顶端 窗体底端
